assertion_results.text: Sibling check correctly classifies TC-8001 as a different-stream companion (stream [rhtpa-2.2] vs current issue's [rhtpa-2.1]), not a same-stream duplicate (Step 4)
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md Step 4.1 explicitly states: 'TC-8006 has stream suffix [rhtpa-2.1] (stream 2.1.x). TC-8001 has stream suffix [rhtpa-2.2] (stream 2.2.x). These are different streams. TC-8001 is NOT a same-stream duplicate.' Step 4.2 then classifies TC-8001 as a 'different-stream sibling (companion tracker)'.

assertion_results.text: Step 4.2 checks the current issue's existing issuelinks before attempting to create a 'Related' link to TC-8001 (§1.58)
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md Step 4.2 includes a 'Pre-existing link check' section that states: 'Per Step 4.2 procedure: Check for existing link before creating one. Read the current issue's issuelinks array from the jira.get_issue response (already fetched in Step 1).' It then enumerates TC-8006's existing issue links from Step 1 data extraction and checks whether they match the two criteria (type.name is Related and outwardIssue.key matches TC-8001).

assertion_results.text: Step 4.2 detects the pre-existing 'Related' link to TC-8001 and skips link creation — does NOT call jira.create_link (§1.58)
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md Step 4.2 states: 'All criteria satisfied. A matching Related link already exists.' followed by 'Skipped link creation.' and 'No jira.create_link call is made. The existing link (ID 1990401) is sufficient for cross-stream coordination. This is the idempotent behavior specified in Step 4.2.'

assertion_results.text: The output includes a log message indicating the link was skipped because it already exists (e.g., 'Related link to TC-8001 already exists — skipping')
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md Step 4.2 logs: 'Related link to TC-8001 already exists -- skipping'. triage-outcome.md also repeats: 'Related link to TC-8001 already exists -- skipping'. The message uses '--' instead of an em dash but conveys the same information.

assertion_results.text: The sibling landscape table is still presented to the engineer despite the link already existing — the idempotent check only affects link creation, not the sibling summary
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md includes a 'Sibling landscape presentation' section after the skipped link creation, containing a table with columns Issue/Stream/Status/Affects Versions showing both TC-8001 (2.2.x, In Progress) and TC-8006 (2.1.x, New). triage-outcome.md confirms: 'Sibling landscape presented to the engineer for awareness.'
